# SCRIPT INVENTORY — paths for every script cited in surveys

**Generated by** `validate-reproducibility-paths` (agent-685, 2026-05-05).
**Convention.** All scripts live under `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/` on the moosefs share. None are vendored into this repo (`/moosefs/erikg/phrs/`); for the manuscript Methods section the absolute paths below are authoritative. The `Verified` column was confirmed by direct `ls` on 2026-05-05.

> **Note on vendoring.** This repo (`/moosefs/erikg/phrs/`) is the manuscript-prep tree, not the analysis tree. The pipeline itself lives in `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/scripts/` and is a shared resource across the project. Per architect guardrails ("no new wet-lab or large new computational analyses"), this audit does **not** copy the pipeline into the manuscript repo; instead every script path cited in any of the 13 `paper_prep/surveys/SURVEY_*.md` documents is enumerated below with verification status. For final publication, copy this directory tree (with the pinned commits in `VERSIONS.md`) into supplementary code or a Zenodo deposit.

---

## 1. Top-level pipeline scripts (`/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/scripts/`)

| Script | Purpose | Cited in | Verified |
|---|---|---|---|
| `classify_contigs.py` | p/q-arm classification of 12,649 contigs from CHM13 centromere coords | SURVEY_01 §1.1, §3 | ✓ |
| `trim-telomeres.sh` | Detect & trim telomeric tracts; emit `.telo.tsv` per assembly | SURVEY_01 §3, SURVEY_02 §5 (telomere TSV upstream) | ✓ |
| `phr_wfmash_array.sh` | Slurm array driver for per-flank wfmash all-vs-all | SURVEY_01 §3 | ✓ |
| `phr_post_wfmash.sh` | Post-wfmash bookkeeping (PAF aggregation) | SURVEY_01 §3 | ✓ |
| `find-multichr-regions-incremental.py` | Inter-chromosomal region scan from per-flank PAFs (PHR detection) | SURVEY_01 §1.4, §3, SURVEY_08 §1 (mouse), SURVEY_10/11/12 §6 | ✓ |
| `find-multichr-regions.py` | Non-incremental variant of the same scan | (referenced only as ancestor) | ✓ |
| `extract_flanking_sequences.py` | Extract 100 kb centromere-ward of PHR boundary (flanking control) | SURVEY_05 §1.7, SURVEY_07 §1 ("flanking paradox"), SURVEY_08 §1, SURVEY_09 §4.5 | ✓ |
| `extract_telo_flanks.mouse.sh` | Mouse-specific flank extraction wrapper | SURVEY_08 §3 | ✓ |
| `trim_mouse_flanks.py` | In-place mouse flank telomere trimming (preserves PanSN) | SURVEY_08 §1, §6 T-3 | ✓ |
| `verify_rpe1_results.py` | RPE-1 global summary extraction | SURVEY_05 §6, SURVEY_09 §1 | ✓ |
| `wfmash.sh` / `wfmash-entries.py` / `wfmash-performance.py` | wfmash drivers and audit | SURVEY_01 §3 (pipeline) | ✓ |
| `partitionPGGB.sh` / `pggb-on-partition.sh` | pggb partition drivers | SURVEY_01 §3 (pipeline) | ✓ |

## 2. Community detection (`scripts/community/`)

| Script | Purpose | Cited in | Verified |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detect_communities.R` | Leiden modularity / silhouette scans (arm-level + sequence-level k×resolution) | SURVEY_01 §1.8, SURVEY_08 §1, SURVEY_09 §1.3, §4.3 | ✓ |
| `community-utils.R` | Shared helpers (used by other community scripts) | SURVEY_01 §3 | ✓ |
| `extract-seq-assignments.R` | Extract sequence-level (50-community) assignments | SURVEY_01 §3 | ✓ |
| `compare-community-levels.R` | Arm-Leiden vs Sequence-Leiden ARI/NMI confusion matrix | SURVEY_01 §3 | ✓ |
| `analyze_hic_communities.py` | Community-based 3-test framework (W/B + bootstrap, Mantel, per-PHR-pair) | SURVEY_05 §6, SURVEY_08 §1.7, SURVEY_09 §1.4 | ✓ |
| `sequence_hic_correlation.py` | Community-free per-sequence-pair Jaccard × Hi-C correlation | SURVEY_05 §6, SURVEY_08 §1, SURVEY_09 §4.5 | ✓ |
| `plot_seqlevel_overlay.py` | Sequence-level overlay scatters | SURVEY_05 §6 | ✓ |
| `parm_qarm_3d_enrichment.py` | p-arm vs q-arm comparison | SURVEY_05 §6 | ✓ |
| `allele_vs_paralog_distance.R` | Allele-vs-paralog Wilcoxon paired test | SURVEY_04 §1.1, §6 | ✓ |
| `analyze-within-arm-heterogeneity.R` | Affinity, population bias, type discordance, gene replacement | SURVEY_04 §1.4, §1.7 | ✓ |
| `plot-within-arm-heterogeneity.R` | 12-panel within-arm heterogeneity plotting | SURVEY_04 §6 | ✓ |
| `compute_fst_superpop.py` | Hudson Fst by superpopulation | SURVEY_04 §1.4, §6 | ✓ |
| `analyze-tar1-positional.R` | TAR1 positional distance-from-telomere | SURVEY_02 §6 | ✓ |
| `analyze-ttaggg-islands.py` | (TTAGGG)n island boundary, length, cross-arm count | SURVEY_02 §6 | ✓ |
| `analyze-ttaggg-motifs.py` | Hexamer motif composition | SURVEY_02 §6 | ✓ |
| `analyze-island-exchange-status.py` | (TTAGGG)n island × exchange-status linkage | SURVEY_02 §6 | ✓ |
| `ttaggg_boundary_enrichment.py` | KS / binomial test for ITS-vs-breakpoint co-localisation | SURVEY_02 §6 | ✓ |
| `telomere_length_by_community.py` | Kruskal-Wallis on terminal telomere length | SURVEY_02 §6 | ✓ |
| `analyze-community-enrichments.R` / `plot-community-enrichments.R` | Enrichment plotting | SURVEY_04 §6 | ✓ |
| `plot_community_composition.py` | Community composition plotting | SURVEY_04 §6 | ✓ (permission-restricted; readable to owner) |

| `compute_its_breakpoint_coloc.py` | Internal-(TTAGGG)n-to-breakpoint distances | SURVEY_04 §1.3 Test 3, SURVEY_10/11/12 §6 | ✓ |

## 11. Repo-root R scripts (`/moosefs/erikg/phrs/.../` — already in this repo)

These are the cytogenetic / gene-enrichment plotting scripts already vendored in the repo root.

| Script | Purpose | Cited in | Verified |
|---|---|---|---|
| `plot-impg-coverage.R` | impg-coverage atlas; producer of `identity_heatmap_chr*.pdf` and `p_combined_*` figures | SURVEY_FIG_inv §1, §2 | ✓ |
| `phr_copy_weighted_enrichment.R` | PHR copy-weighted gene enrichment | (gene-enrichment artefact) | ✓ |
| `improved_copy_weighted_enrichment.R` | Updated copy-weighted enrichment | SURVEY_DATA §4 | ✓ |

The repo root also contains a large suite of statistical-validation / benchmarking R scripts for the gene-enrichment work (~70 files in `R/` and root). These are exhaustive but not directly cited in surveys; treated as ED4 supplementary code.

---

## Cross-reference: paths cited in surveys but NOT a script

The following are **data** paths repeatedly cited as inputs/outputs to the scripts above; they are tracked here so the figure-source-path verification (next section) is unambiguous.

- `/moosefs/pangenomes/HPRCv2/*.fa.gz` — 465 near-complete assemblies (input).
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/pq-classification/contig_classifications.tsv` — 12,649 contigs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/hprcv2.1Mb.telo_500kb_trimmed.fa.gz` — 18,827 flanks.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/hprcv2.1Mb.telo_trimmed.p95.id95.fa.gz` — 15,668 PHRs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/all-vs-all.1Mb.p95.id95.len.tsv` — inter-chromosomal region calls.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/similarity/hprcv2.1Mb.subtelo.{arm-leiden-k15,seq-leiden-k50}.assignments.tsv` — community assignments.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/similarity/hprcv2.1Mb.subtelo.arm_dist_matrix.tsv` — 41×41 arm distances.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/heterogeneity/{allele_vs_paralog_distance,fst_superpop_matrix,cross_arm_affinity_sequences}.tsv` — heterogeneity outputs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/plots/two_domain_{test,changepoint,binned_means,per_sequence}.tsv`, `its_breakpoint_coloc.tsv` — two-domain analysis outputs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/analysis/human/community_based/{res}bp/<sample>_global_test.tsv` — Hi-C global tests, 8 samples × 5 resolutions.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/analysis/human/exclusion_controls/{no_acro_p,no_sex,no_acro_p_sex,no_acro_pq_sex,no_strong}/` — confound exclusion grids.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/RPE1_subtelo/rpe1.{communities,dist_matrix,leiden_scan}.tsv` — RPE-1 self-discovered communities (see §13 below for filename note).
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/mouse_T2T/{community_analysis_1Mb,similarity_1Mb,similarity_2Mb,similarity_4Mb}/...` — mouse pipeline outputs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/pedigrees/{washu,ceph1463/{hifiasm,verkko}}/` — pedigree analysis trees.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/pedigrees/all_pedigrees_patches.tsv` — combined patch table.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/dipc_t2t/output_q0_XX/...` — Dip-C GM12878 outputs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/dipc_t2t/sperm/...` — sperm scHi-C outputs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/dipc_t2t/pbmc_hg19/...` — PBMC Dip-C outputs (hg19 projection).
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/hprc_repeatmasker/*.RepeatMasker.bed.gz` — 465 repeat-mask BEDs.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/ttaggg_analysis/{length_distribution,motif_composition}.tsv` — (TTAGGG)n analysis.
- `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/enrichment/{tar1_positional/tar1_positional_per_arm.tsv,community_tar1_by_arm.tsv}` — TAR1 outputs.

---

## 12. Re-verification of figure-source paths in `MANUSCRIPT_SKELETON.md`

Status convention: **OK** = path exists on disk at the cited (or canonical) location; **OK-canonical** = path file-name pattern resolves to a canonical absolute path (recorded below); **MOVED** = file exists but at a different absolute path than the survey-language implies; **GAP** = file not located on disk at this audit, added to `WORK_DECOMPOSITION.md` `## Gaps`.

### Figure 1 — Population-scale subtelomeric communities

| Panel | Cited source | Resolved absolute path | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1a | `p_genome_wide_identity_heatmap.pdf` (or `_no_inset.pdf`) | `/moosefs/erikg/phrs/.wg-worktrees/agent-685/p_genome_wide_identity_heatmap.pdf`, `..._no_inset.pdf` | OK (in repo root) |
| 1b | `p_genome_wide_numchrom_heatmap.pdf` | `/moosefs/erikg/phrs/.wg-worktrees/agent-685/p_genome_wide_numchrom_heatmap.pdf` | OK (in repo root) |
| 1c | `hprcv2.1Mb.subtelo.arm_dist_matrix.tsv` + `arm-leiden-k15.assignments.tsv` | `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/similarity/hprcv2.1Mb.subtelo.arm_dist_matrix.tsv` (8.8 KB);<br>`/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/similarity/hprcv2.1Mb.subtelo.arm-leiden.communities.tsv` (the **k15 assignments** are stored under name `arm-leiden.communities.tsv`, not `arm-leiden-k15.assignments.tsv` — see Gap 1) | OK-canonical (filename normalisation) |
| 1d | `cross_arm_affinity_sequences.tsv` | `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/heterogeneity/cross_arm_affinity_sequences.tsv` | OK |

## 13. Filename-inconsistency resolution: `rpe1_subtelo.communities.tsv` vs `rpe1.communities.tsv`

**Status: RESOLVED.**

`SURVEY_09 §3.1, §5 #1` flagged that the source markdown referenced `rpe1_subtelo.communities.tsv` and `rpe1_subtelo.dist_matrix.tsv`, while the actual files on disk were `rpe1.communities.tsv` / `rpe1.dist_matrix.tsv`. **Both naming variants now exist on disk** — they are independent runs, not a rename:

| File | Size | mtime | Communities reported |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/RPE1_subtelo/rpe1_subtelo.communities.tsv` | 533 B | 2026-03-20 04:20 | 39 communities (older Leiden run, `rpe1_subtelo.*` prefix) |
| `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/RPE1_subtelo/rpe1.communities.tsv` | 530 B | 2026-03-24 01:18 | 37 communities (current Leiden run, `rpe1.*` prefix; SURVEY_09 §1.3 reports the 37-community result) |

Both files contain the same 47 lines (header + 46 arms). 24/46 arm-community assignments differ between them; the difference is principally the renumbering induced by the slightly different community count (39 vs 37) and the corresponding Leiden resolution-scan tail in `rpe1.leiden_scan.tsv` vs `rpe1_subtelo.leiden_scan.tsv`.

**Resolution adopted (manuscript-canonical):**

- The **`rpe1.*` prefix** (newer, 37 communities) is the authoritative set for the manuscript. SURVEY_09 §1.3 already reports the 37-community number, and `MANUSCRIPT_SKELETON.md` Fig 4c cites `rpe1.dist_matrix.tsv` (consistent with the `rpe1.*` prefix).
- The **`rpe1_subtelo.*` files** are retained for traceability of the earlier Leiden parameter sweep but should NOT be cited in the manuscript or new figures. They will be referenced once, in SI Methods, as "an earlier resolution-sweep result" with the explicit pointer to the canonical files.
- This audit recommends **adding a `README.md` inside `/moosefs/guarracino/HPRCv2/PHR_III/RPE1_subtelo/`** distinguishing the two prefixes; logging this as a follow-up sub-task is out of scope for this validation (no script vendoring or large analysis), but it is added to `WORK_DECOMPOSITION.md` `## Gaps` as a documentation TODO.
- All future references in surveys, figures, and `MANUSCRIPT_SKELETON.md` use **`rpe1.communities.tsv` / `rpe1.dist_matrix.tsv` / `rpe1.leiden_scan.tsv`** as the authoritative names.
